plainEnglish: This is the stricter Level AAA version of 3.3.8: a cognitive function test can't be required at any authentication step unless there's a non-cognitive alternative or an assisting mechanism—and the object-recognition and personal-content exceptions of 3.3.8 are removed. Prefer passwordless methods like passkeys.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities are excluded by any login step requiring memory or puzzle-solving, even the object-recognition CAPTCHAs that are still allowed at Level AA.
quickCheck: Confirm authentication offers a method with no cognitive test at all—such as passkeys, email magic links, or full password-manager support.
commonMistakes: Requiring an object-recognition CAPTCHA (allowed at AA but not at AAA).
Offering only password-based login with no passwordless alternative.
Blocking password managers or paste on credential fields.

codeExamples.label: Passwordless sign-in with no cognitive test
codeExamples.code:
{/* Passkeys require no memory or puzzle-solving */}
<button onClick={signInWithPasskey}>Sign in with a passkey</button>

codeExamples.label: Login gated behind a puzzle CAPTCHA
codeExamples.code:
{/* Requires solving a puzzle to authenticate */}
<PuzzleCaptcha onSolve={continueLogin} />
